description: Detailed errors can reveal secrets, identifiers, existence, or internals.
body: Security-sensitive errors should be useful without leaking sensitive data. Use when secure coding work touches trusted boundaries, dangerous sinks, authorization checks, secret flows, and negative-path tests. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include server-side deny tests, sink-specific payload tests, secret redaction checks, dependency evidence, and audit notes. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves client-only enforcement, secret disclosure, injection, permissive fallback, and unsafe shortcuts, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
